history.text: In 1699, the French built a fort to protect Roseau.
In 1770, the British built Fort Young in place of the previous fort.
In 1778, the French invaded Dominica, capturing Fort Young and Roseau and taking control of the island.

history.label: 1950s
history.text: Goodwill was established in the 1950s and Bath Estate in the early 1980s.

In 2007, Windsor Park, a cricket stadium which is also home to the Dominica national football team, was completed with a donation of EC$33 million from the government of the People's Republic of China.

history.label: Modern city
history.text: With the arrival of the Europeans in the 16th and 17th centuries, a small settlement was established by the French who, in their tradition of naming places after what they found there, used their.

history.label: Today
history.text: A plan was created for the settlement that mirrored examples in France where streets extended from a central point – what is today the Old Market – and spread out to the rest of.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: By the late 18th and early 19th centuries, the British set out plans for the city that included fortifications and government structures, the grid street system, and blocks and new urban areas to the.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Since then several new semi-urban settlements – such as Stock Farm, Castle Comfort, and Wall House – have been constructed around the existing ones.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: From Morne Bruce there are views of the Botanic Gardens at its base as well as the Caribbean Sea which look quite spectacular when cruise liners are in port.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: No other centre in the region has such extensive botanical gardens with such central location, and the Roseau River is among the largest that flow through any Caribbean capital.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The urban structure of Central Roseau is based on an irregular grid system of miniature proportions, making it a highly illegible city.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Around the city there are good examples of the English influence in large colonial town houses and colonial public/government buildings. Religion The churches in Roseau are fine examples of Europe in Dominica with a.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Roman Catholic Cathedral stands prominent in Gothic Romanesque revival and the Anglican Church on Victoria Street in Georgian style.
